mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate cut observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates and recession probabilities rise, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, driving corporate earnings revisions and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or quarterly forward returns are negative in > 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production signal supply chain bottlenecks and margin compression, triggering short-term risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Weekly returns are positive in > 50% of contraction episodes, or the mechanism shows zero incremental predictive value over a simple moving average benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, higher discount rates compress equity multiples and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-rate months, or the threshold effect vanishes when controlling for earnings growth surprises.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate indicates robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer demand and generating positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in > 55% of observations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising household confidence and construction sector activity, which propagate through supply chains to drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with housing starts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all rolling 5-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth exceeds 0.5 percent month-over-month, manufacturing capacity utilization improves and corporate profit margins expand, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Weekly returns are negative in > 50% of threshold-crossing months, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample testing.
